Phase-20.NAME: Configurable VPN Subnets and 1000-Device Capacity
Phase-20.goal: Remove fixed 10.0.0.0/8 VPN addressing assumptions and make KrotPN deployable with address pools sized for 500 and 1000 client devices.

scope.item-1: Fresh deployments must support non-10.0.0.0/8 tunnel networks so KrotPN can coexist with OpenVPN using 10.0.0.0/8.
scope.item-2: Client address pools must be validated against 500/1000-device capacity profiles.
scope.item-3: RU/DE relay addressing, host routes, NAT and policy-routing must use the same selected network settings.
scope.item-4: Existing production clients must not be silently invalidated; live migration requires explicit operator-controlled rotation.

design.choice-1: Recommended fresh-deploy client subnet: 172.29.0.0/22, gateway 172.29.0.1, clients 172.29.0.2 through 172.29.3.254.
design.choice-2: Recommended RU/DE relay subnet: 172.29.255.0/30, DE 172.29.255.1, RU 172.29.255.2.
design.choice-3: Use /22 as the minimum 1000-device profile and allow /21 for headroom beyond 1000 devices.
design.choice-4: Expose VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE as an explicit migration gate because existing AWG client configs embed tunnel addresses.

Phase-20.step-1: Add backend settings and validation helpers for VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET, VPN_CLIENT_GATEWAY, VPN_RELAY_SUBNET, VPN_RELAY_DE_ADDRESS, VPN_RELAY_RU_ADDRESS, VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE and VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE.
Phase-20.step-2: Replace hardcoded 10.10.0.0/24 and 10.200.0.0/24 values in deploy scripts with validated env-derived variables.
Phase-20.step-3: Make VPN client IP allocation capacity-aware and reject subnet/capacity combinations that cannot serve 500/1000 devices.
Phase-20.step-4: Align VPNServer and bootstrap defaults with selected subnets without mutating existing DB rows unless migration is explicit.
Phase-20.step-5: Add deploy preflight checks for overlap with existing host routes, OpenVPN 10.0.0.0/8, Docker networks and selected KrotPN subnets.
Phase-20.step-6: Add explicit live-migration behavior that rotates/reprovisions client configs only when VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1.
Phase-20.step-7: Update routing, NAT, policy-routing, forwarding and RU/DE relay setup to derive all rules from selected client and relay networks.
Phase-20.step-8: Update env templates and operator docs with recommended 172.29.0.0/22 client and 172.29.255.0/30 relay settings.
Phase-20.step-9: Add regression tests and static deploy-script checks that reject hardcoded 10.10/10.200 assumptions outside compatibility notes.
Phase-20.step-10: Run backend and shell verification gates, then mark V-M-032 passed only with command evidence.

data-flows.DF-P20-FRESH-DEPLOY: Operator env to deploy validation to AWG interface addresses to backend VPN allocation to generated client configs.
data-flows.DF-P20-UPGRADE-PRESERVE: Existing deployment to config validation to no network rotation to current DB client addresses and configs remaining valid.
data-flows.DF-P20-EXPLICIT-MIGRATION: VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1 to host route/NAT replacement to client config rotation/reprovisioning to old 10.x assumptions removed.
data-flows.DF-P20-CAPACITY: VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE=1000 to subnet capacity check to /24 rejected and /22 or larger accepted.

phase-gates.gate-1: No fresh deployment path requires 10.0.0.0/8 for KrotPN tunnel addressing.
phase-gates.gate-2: 1000-device profile passes only with a subnet that has at least 1000 usable client addresses after reservations.
phase-gates.gate-3: Upgrade path without VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE does not mutate existing client addresses.
phase-gates.gate-4: RU/DE relay, AWG obfuscation, anti-abuse and manual-device config generation remain compatible after subnet changes.

execution-evidence.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_network_addressing.py tests/test_deploy_network_subnets_static.py tests/test_vpn_service.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py tests/test_device_api.py -v
